subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **1 game board** (central market)
- **120 ship cards** (levels 1-3)
- **12 starting ship cards** (one per sector, per player)
- **5 player boards** (with sectors numbered 1-12)
- **Charge tokens** (blue cubes)
- **Income tokens** (green cubes)
- **Victory point tokens** (yellow cubes)
- **5 dice** (2 standard six-sided dice, per active player)
- **Colony cards** (high-cost, powerful end-game cards)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Place the **central market board** in the center of the table.
2. Separate ship cards into **Level 1**, **Level 2**, and **Level 3** decks. Shuffle each separately.
3. Deal ship cards face-up into the market rows on the board — fill all Level 1 slots, Level 2 slots, and Level 3 slots.
4. Place **Colony cards** in their designated area on the board.
5. Place all charge, income, and VP tokens in a general supply near the board.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player takes a **player board** — it has 12 numbered sectors (1-12).
2. Each player receives their **12 starting ship cards** and places one face-up in each sector (1 through 12). These are your **active** (top) cards.
3. Set your **income tracker** to 0, your **VP tracker** to 0, and your **credits** to 5.
4. The starting player is chosen randomly. In turn order, players 2-5 receive 1 additional starting credit each (player 2 gets 6 credits, player 3 gets 7, etc.).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Space Base is a dice-activation engine-building game. On **your** turn, you roll two dice and activate sectors on your board. On **other players' turns**, their dice rolls can also trigger your deployed (flipped) cards.

The goal is to be the first player to reach **40 victory points**. You do this by purchasing ship cards from the market and strategically placing them to maximize rewards from every dice roll — yours and your opponents'.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, follow these steps:

### 1. Roll Dice
Roll both dice. You may use the dice in one of two ways:
- **Combined**: Add both dice together and activate that single sector (e.g., 3 + 4 = sector 7).
- **Split**: Activate each die's sector separately (e.g., sector 3 AND sector 4).

### 2. Activate Sectors
- **Active cards** (top, face-up on your board): You gain their rewards on **your** turn.
- **Deployed cards** (bottom, tucked under face-down): You gain their rewards on **other players'** turns.

Rewards include: **credits** (money), **income** (permanent boost to credits earned each turn), **victory points**, or **special abilities**.

### 3. Collect Income
Gain credits equal to your current income level.

### 4. Buy a Card (Optional)
You may purchase **one** ship card from the market using credits:
- Place the new card **face-up** in its matching sector (active position).
- The old card in that sector gets **flipped face-down** and tucked underneath (deployed position).
- Deployed cards now trigger when **opponents** roll that number.

### 5. Refill Market
Refill any empty market slots from the appropriate deck.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Buying Ships
- Each ship card has a **sector number** (1-12) and a **cost** in credits.
- Level 1 cards are cheap but weak. Level 2 and 3 cards are expensive but powerful.
- When placed, the previous card in that sector becomes **deployed** — now it activates on opponents' rolls.

### Card Rewards
- **Arrow up (credits)**: Gain credits immediately.
- **Arrow right (income)**: Increase your income permanently.
- **Star (VP)**: Gain victory points.
- **Special abilities**: Draw cards, steal, charge effects, etc.

### Charge Abilities
Some cards have **charge** abilities. When activated, place a charge token on them. You can spend charges for powerful one-time effects on future turns.

### Colony Cards
Colony cards cost a large amount of credits but provide massive VP and powerful ongoing effects. These are your late-game power plays.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Other Players' Turns
When another player rolls dice, **all other players** also activate their **deployed** (bottom) cards for those numbers. This is what makes the game unique — you benefit from every roll at the table.

### Choosing Combined vs. Split
You always choose whether to use dice combined or split. This applies both on your own turn (for active cards) and on opponents' turns (for deployed cards).

### Sector 1
Sector 1 can only be activated by splitting dice when one die shows a 1. It cannot be activated by combining.

### Sectors 7-12
These sectors can only be activated by combining dice (you can't roll higher than 6 on a single die).

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
The game ends immediately when a player reaches **40 victory points**. That player wins.

- VP are tracked on your player board throughout the game.
- There is no final scoring — all points are earned during play.
- If multiple players would reach 40 on the same roll (from deployed cards), the **active player** (the one who rolled) wins ties.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Prioritize income early**. Boosting your income means more credits every turn, letting you buy better ships sooner.
- **Sectors 6, 7, and 8** are statistically most likely to be rolled. Focus upgrades there first.
- **Don't neglect deployed cards**. They trigger on every opponent's turn — in a 5-player game, that's 4 activations per round.
- **Buy Level 2 and 3 cards** as soon as you can afford them. Their rewards are dramatically better.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Deployed card engine**: In higher player counts, deployed cards trigger more often. Prioritize getting strong cards deployed in high-probability sectors.
- **Credit vs. VP balance**: Early game, focus on credits and income. Mid-game, start converting to VP-generating cards. Late game, look for big VP swings.
- **Colony rush**: If you can generate enough credits quickly, Colony cards offer huge VP chunks that can end the game fast.
- **Spread vs. focus**: Spreading upgrades across many sectors gives consistency. Focusing on a few sectors creates big swing turns but is less reliable.
- **Watch opponents**: If someone is close to 40 VP, avoid rolling numbers that activate their strong deployed cards (you can't control dice, but you can choose combined vs. split strategically).
